summary: maplibre-gl-vue3 republishes maplibre-gl verbatim under a misleading name
details: Package maplibre-gl-vue3@5.24.3 ships a verbatim copy of upstream maplibre-gl v5.24.3 source and dist bundle. The name implies a Vue 3 integration/wrapper, but no Vue-specific code is present: src/index.ts re-exports the plain maplibre-gl API (Map, NavigationControl, etc.), and the inner dist/package.json declares name 'maplibre-gl' with a 'deprecated' hint pointing to the parent. No install/preinstall/postinstall/prepare lifecycle scripts are declared, no network I/O to non-first-party hosts is performed, no credential reads or obfuscated execution paths are present, and no self-propagation or dependency-tree hijack behavior is present. The package does not attack installers, but the name misrepresents contents (users expecting a Vue 3 wrapper receive the base library) and could function as a confusion/squat against the maplibre-gl namespace.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
